disclaimer: Operational compliance research and cited public-records compilation — not legal, tax, or accounting advice. This package records what public .gov sources show as of the retrieval date; confirm licensing, bonding, permit, and code requirements with the relevant state or local authority and licensed counsel before relying on any fact for filings or operations.

competitive_positioning.stonebridge_angle: 50-state cited .gov compliance bible — every fact sourced, every gap explicit (NOT FOUND / REQUIRES_CONFIRMATION), federal OSHA/EPA/ICC baseline included; monthly source-register refresh available via RCaaS.

sections.title: Federal Baseline (OSHA · EPA · ICC · NASCLA)
sections.purpose: Cross-cutting federal construction standards that apply in every state before state-specific licensing, bonding, permits, and code adoption.
sections.gap_policy: Federal seed facts from #234 cited_data_pack; harvested facts carry origin tag. No inference beyond cited text.

sections.title: State Licensing by Trade
sections.purpose: Per-state regulator identity, statewide GC model, NASCLA participation, and cited facts for GC, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, and roofing.

expected_datums.label: Statewide GC license required (yes / no / local-only / HIC-only)

sections.gap_policy: Interactive portals without API → ACCESS_RESTRICTED with portal URL cited; never infer license status or fees.

sections.title: Bonding & Insurance
sections.purpose: State-level bonding, surety, and insurance requirements cited from public regulator pages.

sections.title: Permits & Inspections
sections.purpose: State permit authority, inspection workflow, and cited public requirements.

sections.title: Building Codes
sections.purpose: Statewide code adoption (UCC, I-Codes, amendments) from cited public code authority pages.

sections.title: Source Register Appendix
sections.purpose: Complete register of .gov sources harvested for this lane — federal (#234) and state (#266) — for audit and monthly refresh.
sections.gap_policy: PARKED sources (HTTP 403/404) listed with park_reason; never bypassed.
